rulings.Case Title: DICKERMAN VS ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BHC)*
rulings.Rulings: GRANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RE EXPOSURE. COURT AGREES THAT PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY DOES NOT SHIFT THE BURDEN. SEE CHAKNOVA V. WILBER-ELLIS CO., (1999) 69 CAL.APP.4TH 962, 975-976, FN. 12. HOWEVER, THE DRIVER DECLARATION DEMONSTRATES THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT PRODUCE ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT CONTROVERTING EVIDENCE OR DEMONSTRATE SUCCESSOR LIABILITY. (PB)

rulings.Case Title: THOMASON VS TRADEGATEINTERNATIONAL.COM
rulings.Rulings: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION NON PAYMENT OF WAGES, 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION WAITING TIME PENALTY, 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION BREACH OF EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT, 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT TREATED AS MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AND GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE PARTNERSHIP OR PROMOTER LIABILITY. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION WRONGFUL TERMINATION GRANTED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION, 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION, AND 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION DENIED. (AA)

rulings.Case Title: GROESSLER VS ACKERMANN
rulings.Rulings: DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON LEGAL MALPRACTICE. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO CONTINUED LEGAL REPRESENTATION. DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH. DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION BASED ON FRAUD PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION (PARAGRAPH 11) STATES DAMAGES EXIST. "ACKERMANN HAS IMPROPERLY ENRICHED HIMSELF WITH $3 MILLION OF WHAT WAS, RIGHTFULLY, MY MONEY." (AA)
